sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: People with suspected or confirmed endometriosis, including adolescents, with recommendation-specific fertility and imaging contexts.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, gynaecology and specialist endometriosis assessment and management.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for recorded symptom pattern, impact, examination, clinician-selected ultrasound or referral, and follow-up of an established plan. It does not prove endometriosis, exclude other causes, select treatment or define UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — signs and symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports chronic pelvic pain, dysmenorrhoea affecting activities, deep dyspareunia, bowel or urinary cyclical symptoms, infertility and quality-of-life context without diagnosing endometriosis.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.5.8 — examination and ultrasound
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed abdominal or pelvic examination and recommendation-qualified transvaginal or transabdominal ultrasound while recognizing that a normal examination or scan does not exclude endometriosis.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.9 onward — referral and management pathway
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-decided referral, preference discussion and review in qualified contexts without selecting treatment or urgency automatically.
